title: Add viewer-shell support for rich/raw toggles, search highlighting, and preview caps
description:
Add the shared `DocumentViewer` shell behavior needed by the new modular renderers without turning the viewer into a format-specific implementation layer.

## Background

The new CSV and text/code renderers need some shared viewer behavior: inline rich/raw switching, consistent search-query propagation, and capped-preview UX for large files. That behavior should be driven by renderer capabilities so the viewer shell stays generic and existing Markdown and HTML renderers remain stable.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `DocumentViewer` supports inline rich/raw toggling for renderers that advertise that capability.
- [ ] Search queries propagate correctly to the new renderers without regressing existing Markdown behavior.
- [ ] Preview-cap behavior is wired through the viewer shell in a renderer-agnostic way.
- [ ] Existing HTML behavior remains intact, including the current history suppression and iframe-based rendering path.
- [ ] Integration tests cover viewer-shell behavior across Markdown, HTML, CSV, and text/code renderers.

## Notes

This task should focus on shared shell behavior rather than implementing CSV parsing or source rendering details directly in `DocumentViewer`.
